bull.b: Sustained top-line growth and a rapidly expanding subscription base (subscription revenue +38% YoY; 55% of revenue) improve predictability and recurring cash flows. Durable subscription expansion supports upsell, reduces revenue volatility, and underpins margin leverage over multiple quarters.

bull.t: Strong Free Cash Flow And Deleveraging
bull.b: Robust operating and free cash flow generation alongside a sharp fall in gross/net debt provides lasting financial flexibility. High FCF enables reinvestment in product and sales, funds continued deleveraging, and reduces refinancing risk, strengthening long-term capital allocation options.

Rapid AI adoption and embedded workflows create differentiated products and measurable ROI, boosting win rates and larger deals. Combined with broad integrations and transaction scale, this builds durable competitive moats through data advantage, faster feature learning, and higher retention.

bear.t: Implementation Backlog And Long Revenue Ramps
bear.b: Extended implementation timelines for bigger, multi-module contracts delay revenue realization and create execution risk. Persistent backlog can compress near-term growth, pressure working capital needs, and increase dependency on flawless project delivery to convert bookings into durable revenue.

A meaningful revenue slice faces secular shifts (print-to-digital conversion) and seasonality that can lower reported volume and cash flow timing. Structural pressure in this segment reduces topline predictability and forces product/price adaptation to preserve long-term unit economics.

bear.t: Historic Margin Volatility And Modest ROE
bear.b: While profitability has improved, prior margin swings and only modest ROE imply the company must sustain execution to convert scale into high returns. If cost or mix pressures re-emerge, ability to generate best-in-class ROE remains an open structural risk over the medium term.
